title: chroot fails with EPERM in storage-applyLayer on FreeBSD
body:
# How to reproduce

* Check that `sysctl kern.chroot_allow_open_directories` is set to the default value 1
* Install podman in a FreeBSD jail
* Run `podman import /path/to/tar testimage`

Alternatively (not tested) it should be possible to reproduce without a jail:

* Set `sysctl kern.chroot_allow_open_directories=0`
* Run `podman import /path/to/tar testimage`

# Workaround

When setting sysctl `kern.chroot_allow_open_directories` to 2, import succeeds even when running in a jail.

# Conclusion

There are open filedescriptors to other directories by the time that `chroot` is called. I suspect little effort to fix this, however not sure.

Is there a way to trick podman into running `podman storage-applyLayer` with the dlv debugger so I could further investigate and find the offending open directories?
